title: The ph login command generates a did:key keypair and delegates it to act on behalf of an Ethereum address via Renown
content: Running `ph login` generates a cryptographic keypair (ECDSA P-256) stored in `.keypair.json`, creates a DID in `did:key:...` format, opens the browser to Renown portal for the user to authorize the CLI DID to act on behalf of their Ethereum address, and stores credentials in `.auth.json`. Identity files are per-project (not global), enabling different identities per project. The Switchboard uses identity via `ph switchboard --use-identity` to authenticate with remote drives (generate bearer tokens), sign operations, and present its DID to remote services. CI/CD environments can provide the keypair via `PH_RENOWN_PRIVATE_KEY` environment variable. The CLI DID cannot sign Ethereum transactions or access the wallet -- it can only authenticate with Renown-trusting services.
description: CLI creates ECDSA P-256 keypair stored in .keypair.json, opens browser for wallet authorization, stores credentials per-project.
